name: Pineapple-Mint Sorbet
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Strained fresh orange juice (from about 2 lbs oranges)
1 Ripe pineapple, peeled and cored, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the sorbet: Combine sugar and water in a large saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool.
2. Meanwhile, combine orange juice and lime juice.
3. Puree pineapple with mint leaves using a food processor fitted with a steel blade or blender. Add some orange juice to moisten the mixture.
4. Combine this pureed mixture with the cooled syrup.
5. Freeze in an ice cream freezer or cover bowl tightly and freeze. When frozen solid, process in a food processor fitted with the steel blade until smooth (this breaks up ice crystals for a smoother texture). Repeat if desired after a couple of hours.
6. Spoon into individual serving dishes or a loaf pan/mold lined with plastic wrap. Cover tightly with foil and freeze.
7. Garnish: Toss chopped pineapple, optional Cointreau, and mint leaves together. Add to each serving before eating.
This sorbet will keep for weeks in the freezer, and the garnish can be made ahead of time and refrigerated.
